chunk_text: The total issue size is ₹11,300 crores comprising a fresh issue of ₹4,499 crores and an offer for sale of ₹6,801 crores. The price band is set at ₹371 to ₹390 per equity share.

The net proceeds from the fresh issue will be utilized for: (a) investment in material subsidiary Scootsy Logistics for capex and working capital (₹1,179 crores), (b) investment in technology and cloud infrastructure (₹982 crores), (c) brand marketing and business promotion (₹929 crores), and (d) general corporate purposes.

The company has reported net losses of ₹2,350 crores for FY2024 and ₹4,179 crores for FY2023. The company may not achieve profitability in the near future. Continued losses may adversely affect the company's financial condition.

The promoters of the company are Sriharsha Majety, Nandan Reddy, and Rahul Jaimini. Post-issue, the promoter and promoter group will hold approximately 5.37% of the fully diluted equity share capital of the company.

Revenue from operations for FY2024 was ₹11,247 crores, compared to ₹8,265 crores for FY2023, representing a year-on-year growth of 36.1%. EBITDA margin improved from -28.4% to -14.7% over the same period.

Swiggy operates across three primary business segments: food delivery, quick commerce (Instamart), and out-of-home consumption. The company serves over 500 cities across India with a network of approximately 2,85,000 delivery partners.

The food delivery market in India is highly competitive. The company competes primarily with Zomato in food delivery. The quick commerce segment faces competition from Blinkit (a Zomato subsidiary), Zepto, and BigBasket.

During FY2024, the company entered into related party transactions with Prosus NV and its affiliates aggregating to ₹124 crores. All transactions were on an arm's-length basis and disclosed in the financial statements.

The board of directors comprises 9 members, including 4 independent directors. The audit committee is chaired by an independent director. The company has adopted a dividend distribution policy in compliance with SEBI Listing Obligations.

The objects of the issue include augmenting the company's capital base for future growth, providing liquidity to existing shareholders through the offer for sale component, and meeting expenses incidental to the issue. The company will not receive any proceeds from the offer for sale component.
